must_detect.matched_text:
ce covers personal data processing for listed-company workflows under Indian data protection principles and the securities law framework on unpublished price sensitive information (UPSI) and insider trading prohibitions.
We collect business identifiers (name, role, official email), device/usage logs, and, where required for compliance/KYC, masked government identifiers (e.g., last 4 digits of Aadhaar/PAN/GSTIN); we process children’s data (under 18 years) only with verified guardian consent.
Purposes include regulatory compliance, contract performance with vendors (e.g., Meridian Fintech Services Pvt. Ltd.), security monitoring, and investor-relations operations; optional communications rely on consent and can be withdrawn at any time via privacy@aurivastgreen.example.in.
UPSI examples include non-public ESG emissions restatement drafts, commercial terms in sustainability-linked PPA proposals (e.g., tiered pricing and volume-linked discounts), and preliminary cyber incident assessments affecting production OT networks.
Crypto context: non-public evaluations of a green-token custody and settlement pilot with Kaveri Digital Exchange, and treasury wallet integration prototypes, are embargoed and limited to the insider list until public disclosure.
Insider lists are maintained for persons with a legitimate purpose (including Ms. Rhea Kulkarni, Head–Sustainability, and Mr. Dev Malhotra, CIO), with access ring-fenced in the “Project Neem” clean-room; tipping and circumvention of information barriers are strictly prohibited.
Blackout windows apply from quarter-end until 48 hours after results are posted on the Varanasi Stock Exchange, and any material information must be disseminated broadly through exchange releases; selective disclosure to analysts, lenders, or channel partners is not permitted.
Cyber incident workflow: system log data, IP telemetry, and ticketing records may be processed; draft breach notices and ransomware negotiation options are treated as UPSI until disclosed; incident log retention is extended for the duration of investigations.
Educational/marketing: our ESG and cyber academy offers webinars on blockchain wallets, emission baselining, and “insider list” record-keeping as generic training, without reference to live transactions, embargo periods, or any deal-stage materials.
Data subject rights: you may access, correct, port, erase, or restrict processing, and withdraw consent; submit requests to privacy@aurivastgreen.example.in with reasonable identity verification.
Grievance officer: please contact Dr. Nikhil Varma at grievances@aurivastgreen.example.in; children’s requests will be actioned only upon guardian verification consistent with Indian requirements.
Sharing and transfers: we engage processors such as Meridian Fintech Services Pvt. Ltd. and host certain services in the Republic of Navindi; cross-border transfers occur under contractual safeguards, limited to necessity, and subject to Indian legal allowances.
Retention: insider lists and UPSI access logs are retained for 7 years; crypto wallet test artifacts for 18 months; customer support tickets for 24 months; otherwise we minimize retention unless a legal hold or exchange rule requires longer.
Security: role-based access, encryption in transit/at rest, dual control for wallet keys, and network segregation between the Treasury–Crypto Desk and Investor Relations preserve confidentiality and information barriers.
